human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for beauty-specific scheduling boundaries, source quality, provider-scope and med-spa handling, sanitation and readiness controls, scenario coverage, commercialization notes, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to beauty appointment scheduling for service menus, duration matrices, provider-scope checks, room or station readiness, sanitation gating, and waitlist or rebooking support and does not claim licensed, clinical, financial-override, or final approval authority.

Authority sources cover beauty-specific TDLR operating guidance, APQC planning workflow, and OSHA and EPA safety and sanitation controls, so workflow, terminology, evidence, and failure handling are materially changed by the named industry.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete prerequisites, capacity overload, priority conflict, generic-calendar rejection, medical-spa adjacency, same-day callout sequencing, systems-of-record conflict, source-policy conflict, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/beauty-wellness-and-personal-care/appointment-scheduling-beauty-specialist/ so beauty scheduling remains separated from licensed-practice, med-spa, facilities, refund, and safety lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around clinical adjacency, provider-scope assignment, fee waivers, safety overrides, and unsupported booking commitments.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated beauty appointment scheduling specialist that supports evidence-backed booking, capacity, routing, and readiness control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
